name: Notify stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams with instant alerts

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09067-order`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Route by Work Type

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: standard, priority + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'standard' → Google Sheets · ②→ Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert · ③→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'standard' → Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Next: Merge Routed Work

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fast-Track 'priority' + Alert

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Next: Merge Routed Work

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Next: Merge Routed Work

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

# Notify stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams with instant alerts

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Notify stakeholders on changes with Microsoft Teams with instant alerts. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual cart recovery is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Route by Work Type → Process 'standard' → Google Sheets → Merge Routed Work → Align Field Names → Tag Pipeline Metadata

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating cart recovery in e-commerce
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Standardizes the process every time
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your cart recovery process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
